name: stalwart-clean
query: Research FIZZ (Evergreen Beverages) using the given facts.

given_facts.business: Global maker of branded non-alcoholic drinks; household-name products bought repeatedly.

expected_behavior: Classifies FIZZ as a Stalwart (big, established, steady high-single-digit/low-double-digit earnings growth, pays a dividend).
Computes P/E ~25 (78 / 3.10) and a PEG well above 1 (P/E ~25 vs ~9% growth), and states the numbers came from compute_valuation.py, not mental math.
Identifies the central question for a stalwart as PRICE: the P/E is rich relative to growth, so the stock is fully valued.
Notes the dividend-adjusted PEG (growth + yield) / PE = (9 + ~2) / 25 is well below 1 — poor on Lynch's scale.
Does NOT call FIZZ a tenbagger candidate; sets realistic stalwart expectations (~30-50% over time, downside protection).
Verdict is Watch (quality business, but wait for a better price / market fear), not a reflexive Buy because it's a good company.
Includes a bear case (e.g., a stretched multiple, slowing volumes).
Every figure cites a source + as-of date.
